english: the well-disposed, esp. the good spirits, nág-p̊yogs (nag phyogs), sdig-càn-gyi p̊yogs (sdig can gyi phyogs) the bad, malicious, esp. the evil spirits, devils. - 5. in popular language the word is used also with respect to time: *k̇a-sań-stón-čogs* (*kha sang ston chogs*) Ld., last autumn.

I. vb. to turn vb. n., čós-la (chos la) to turn to religion Schr.; p̊yír p̊yógs-pa (phyir phyogs pa) to turn one's self back, to turn aside (Schr. p̊yír p̊yógs-par byéd-pa (phyir phyogs par byed pa), to divert from, to dissuade from) Tar. 12, 14 28, 9. čí-k̇ar p̊yógs-pa (chi khar phyogs pa) turned to dying - near dying? k̇ór-ba-la rgyáb-kyis p̊yógs-pa (khor ba la rgyab kyis phyogs pa), to turn one's back to the orb of transmigration; mńón-du p̊yógs-pa (mngon du phyogs pa), 1. to be visible, to be evident, to be exposed to view(?), lho-ńos-su mńón-du p̊yógs-pai brág-las b̊yúń-ba (lho ngos su mngon du phyogs pai brag las byung ba) growing on a surface rock on the south-side Sambh.; don de mńón-du p̊yógs-par byá-bai p̊yír (don de mngon du phyogs par bya bai phyir), in order to bring this meaning to the light, to express it clearly Gram.(?). 2. to be openly or evidently attached to, to adhere to(?) rgyúd-la (rgyud la) to a Tantra or treatise Sambh. II. adj., sbst., attached to, following; a partizan, an adherent.
Cs. progress, p̊yód čé-ba (phyod che ba), great progress; Lex.: sa-p̊yod-če v. čod (sa phyod che v. chod).

ornament (?), jewel (?) p̊ra rgyág-pa (phra rgyag pa), rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa), gód-pa (god pa), ₒdébs-pa ('debs pa), Sch. also p̊ras sprá-ba (phras spra ba), to insert an ornament of jewels, to stud with jewels; rmog-la pad-ma-rā-gai p̊ra btáb-pa de (rmog la pad ma rA gai phra btab pa de), this set of rubies on the helmet, this helmet studded with rubies Glr.; rín-čén sna-ts̊ógs-kyis p̊ra bkód-pa (rin chen sna tshogs kyis phra bkod pa) Mil.; p̊ra-ts̊óm (phra tshom) border, trimming, Lex.
Was. (241)=bág-la nyál-ba (bag la nyal ba), vanities, i.e. passions, errors, erroneous notions.

1. v. ₒp̊ra-ba ('phra ba). - 2. Lt. a disease of children. - 3. adj., gen. p̊rá-mo (phra mo) (Cs. also bo (bo)) thin, fine, minute, opp. to sbóm-po (sbom po) q.v., sbrul p̊rá-mo żig (sbrul phra mo zhig) Tar.; in a general sense, little, small, séms-càn p̊rá-mo-rnáms (sems can phra mo rnams); nú-p̊ra-mo (nu phra mo), little as to age, young, Mil.; trifling, little, slight, rnám-rtog p̊rá-mo (rnam rtog phra mo) slight scruples, Mil.; rdzun p̊rá-mo (rdzun phra mo), a little lie, a fib, Thgy.; *żíd-mo-nẹ tóń-wa* (*zhid mo ne thong wa*), *láb-pa* (*lab pa*), to see, to inspect most accurately, to learn the minutest details, C.; thin, high, rel. to voice W.; p̊ra-żíb (phra zhib) Lex., fine and exact; šin-tu p̊rá-ba (shin tu phra ba), in reference to the doctrine of Buddha, implying prob. its subtilties. Cf. p̊ran (phran).

calumny, slander, esp. through tell-tales and intermeddling persons B. and col.; p̊rá-ma byéd-pa (phra ma byed pa) Dzl., smrá-ba (smra ba) Cs., j̇úg-pa ('jug pa) B. and C., *čó-čẹ* (*cho che*) W., resp. (when referring to a person of higher rank) γsól-ba (gsol ba), żú-ba (zhu ba), to calumniate, slander, vilify, blacken; p̊rá-ma-mk̇an (phra ma mkhan) Cs. calumniator, slanderer.

sorcery, witchcraft Schr.; so prob. Pth.: mi-dgos-pai p̊ra-mén-gyi ńan-sńágs (mi dgos pai phra men gyi ngan sngags), an evil magic spell of pernicious necromancy; p̊ra-men-po (phra men po) and -pa (-pa) masc., -mo (-mo) and -ma (-ma) fem., necromancer, wizard, witch; p̊ra-mén rdzá-ki (phra men rdza ki) (for dzo-gi (dzo gi), S: योगिन्) id.
v. p̊rá-ba (phra ba); ཕྲ་ཤགས་ p̊ra-šags (phra shags), v. ₒp̊rá-ba ('phra ba).

provinc. also dbrag (dbrag), srag (srag), 1. intermediate space, interstice, interval, hence p̊rág-tu (phrag tu) = bár-du (bar du) Thgy.; a hollow, ravine, defile; smin-p̊rág (smin phrag) v. smin-ma (smin ma). - 2. after cardinal numbers it seems to correspond about to the Greek subst. termination ας: bču-p̊rág (bcu phrag) a decade, brgya-p̊rág (brgya phrag) a hundred (century), stoń-p̊rág (stong phrag) a thousand (chiliad), brgya-p̊rág rčig (brgya phrag gcig), brgya-p̊rág bčú (brgya phrag bcu); stoń-p̊rág bżi-bču-żig (stong phrag bzhi bcu zhig), a number of forty thousand Dzl.; bdun-p̊rág (bdun phrag), ἑβδομάς, week (recognized as a measure of time, but in common life not much in use).

1. sbst., resp. sku-p̊rág (sku phrag) shoulder, p̊rág-pa-la gél-ba (phrag pa la gel ba) Glr., tógs-pa (thogs pa) Sambh. to load on one's shoulder; grógs-poi p̊rág-pa-la ₒdzég-pa (grogs poi phrag pa la 'dzeg pa), to mount the shoulder of one's companion Dzl.; upper arm, p̊rág-pa rnyís-kyi ša (phrag pa rnyis kyi sha) Dzl., p̊rag-góń (phrag gong)
